segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in black and white, accompanied by subtle sounds of footsteps and rustling. On-screen text identifies the group as 'Pitch Slapped' and the event as the '2014 International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella,' with the Berklee College of Music logo visible. A male voice then announces, "Hello, folks. Thank you for tuning into Pitch Slapped Radio Live," before a rhythmic a cappella vocal percussion intro begins. A male lead performer, dressed in a white shirt, black tie, and suspenders, sings passionately with expressive gestures, delivering the lines, "Do you mind if we take our time? 'Cause I've been here before and it nearly took my life." A female lead performer beside him, in a black dress and statement necklace, mirrors his energy with expressive hand movements. As the black-and-white filter transitions to full color, revealing the wooden floor, arched doorways, and a large clock of the rehearsal studio, the full group of performers joins in, providing rich harmonies and synchronized dance moves, including spins and coordinated arm gestures. The male lead continues to deliver the main melody, supported by the ensemble's intricate vocal arrangements and rhythmic backing, creating an energetic and theatrical performance that concludes with more vocal percussion.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens in black and white, showing a group of young performers in a rehearsal space, dressed in coordinated outfits—men in white shirts, black ties, and suspenders; women in black dresses or tops with accessories like statement necklaces. The scene begins with a close-up of a male performer singing passionately, gesturing with his arms, while a female performer beside him mirrors his energy with expressive hand movements. The camera pans across the group, capturing synchronized movements and interactions, suggesting a choreographed a cappella performance. On-screen text identifies the group as 'Pitch Slapped' and the event as the '2014 International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella,' with the Berklee College of Music logo visible. As the video progresses, the black-and-white filter transitions to full color, revealing more details: the wooden floor, arched doorways, and a large clock on the wall. The group performs dynamic, synchronized dance moves, including spins and coordinated arm gestures, with the lead male and female performers often at the forefront. The atmosphere is energetic and theatrical, with the performers fully engaged in their routine. The lighting remains consistent, highlighting the performers against the neutral-toned studio backdrop.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens in a brightly lit, spacious rehearsal room with wooden floors and white walls, featuring arched doorways and a large round clock. A mixed-gender ensemble, the Group of Performers, dressed in coordinated outfits—men in white shirts, black ties, and suspenders; women in black tops or dresses with varied accessories—executes dynamic choreography. The Male Lead Performer, in a white shirt, black tie, and suspenders, and the Female Lead Performer, in a black dress with a prominent statement necklace, take center stage, leading the group with synchronized dance and vocal expressions. Throughout, the Berklee College of Music Logo is visible in the bottom right. The audio features a lively, rhythmic vocalization of 'la-la-la' from the group, creating a vibrant, almost percussive musical backdrop. As the performance progresses, the camera pans and zooms to capture close-ups of individual performers, including a young woman with blonde hair in a black top and white skirt, who becomes a focal point, smiling and gesturing expressively. The atmosphere is joyful, with performers laughing and engaging with each other, their enthusiastic 'la-la-la's continuing to drive the energetic performance.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a vibrant, high-energy a cappella performance by a mixed-gender ensemble in a spacious rehearsal studio with wooden floors and white walls. The Group of Performers, dressed in coordinated outfits, execute synchronized dance moves and vocal harmonies, delivering a professionally produced a cappella track featuring prominent lead vocals, intricate backing harmonies, and dynamic vocal percussion. The Female Lead Performer, in a black dress and statement necklace, takes center stage with expressive gestures and a powerful lead vocal, singing lines like "this song will keep you grooving" and "this song's the one." The Male Lead Performer, in a white shirt and suspenders, also delivers a strong lead vocal and dynamic choreography. As the camera pans across the group, highlighting their energetic movements and joyful expressions, the Female Performer in Black Top and White Skirt becomes a prominent focal point, smiling broadly and gesturing expressively as she dances, contributing to the ensemble's sound. The Berklee College of Music Logo remains visible in the bottom right corner, anchoring the performance to the institution, while the Studio Environment with its arched doorways and a large round clock provides a clean, professional backdrop. Towards the end of the minute, the male lead vocal takes prominence, singing "I don't mind letting you down easy, but just give it time. If it don't hurt now, just wait, just wait a while," maintaining the energetic a cappella style. The group transitions through various formations, including circular and staggered lines, maintaining tight synchronization and enthusiasm, culminating in a final, unified pose that emphasizes their cohesion and joy, all set to their powerful vocal performance.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a dynamic a cappella performance by a group of young performers in a spacious rehearsal studio. The Male Lead Performer, dressed in a white shirt and suspenders, immediately takes a prominent vocal role, delivering rhythmic and melodic lines like "You're not the big fish in the pond no more," while executing dynamic choreography. He is soon joined by the Female Lead Performer, in a black dress, who shares lead vocals with a powerful, melodic voice, particularly during the catchy chorus, "What are you gonna do when the world don't orbit around you." The entire Group of Performers, dressed in coordinated outfits, provides rich, synchronized harmonies and intricate vocal percussion, creating a full, energetic soundscape that drives their fluid, synchronized dance moves. As the camera pans, capturing their joyful expressions and precise arm movements, the Female Performer in Black Top and White Skirt emerges as a visual focal point, smiling broadly and contributing to the group's vibrant vocal tapestry, especially during the "ain't it fine" and "ain't it good to be on your own" sections. The atmosphere is celebratory, with bright lighting highlighting their movements, all while the Berklee College of Music Logo remains visible, anchoring this exuberant display of musical unity and precision.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a spacious rehearsal studio with wooden floors and white walls, featuring arched doorways and a large round clock, all bathed in natural lighting. On-screen text 'Pitch Slapped' and '2014 International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella' appears briefly before fading. A group of young performers, dressed in coordinated outfits—men in white shirts, black ties, and suspenders; women in black tops or dresses—execute dynamic, synchronized dance moves and powerful a cappella vocalizations. The Male Lead Performer and Female Lead Performer take center stage, leading the choreography with expressive arm gestures and facial expressions, singing the repeated chorus, "Don't go crying to your mama, 'cause you're on your own in the real world." The camera fluidly captures close-ups and wide shots, emphasizing the group’s energy and cohesion. Around the 0:41 mark, the song shifts, and the Female Lead Performer takes a solo, singing a rapid, rhythmic line, "I'll take me out, I'll take me out, I'll take back that, I'll take back that, I'll take me out," while the group provides vocal percussion and harmonies. The Male Lead Performer then interjects with spoken words, "Come on y'all," "Pitch Slapped," and "Let's party," before the group launches into an energetic vocal percussion and harmony section. The Female Performer in Black Top and White Skirt becomes a prominent focal point in the latter half, smiling and gesturing expressively, contributing to the lively and joyful atmosphere. The Berklee College of Music Logo remains visible in the bottom right corner throughout, anchoring the performance to the institution, as the performers laugh and engage directly with the camera, creating an immersive and celebratory experience.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a spacious, well-lit rehearsal studio with wooden floors and white walls, featuring the Berklee College of Music Logo consistently visible in the bottom right corner, and brief on-screen text identifying 'Pitch Slapped' and the '2014 International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella' before fading. A dynamic a cappella performance immediately begins with the Group of Performers providing rhythmic vocalizations and beatboxing, creating a full, percussive soundscape. The Male Lead Performer, in a white shirt, black tie, and suspenders, takes center stage, singing lead with expressive facial gestures and energetic arm movements, delivering lyrics like "There's not too many, no one but you in crowded rooms, we can do anything" in a strong tenor voice. He is joined by the Female Lead Performer, in a black dress with a statement necklace, who mirrors his energy with synchronized dance moves and expressive hand gestures, while the rest of the group, dressed in coordinated outfits, executes tight, synchronized dance routines and provides rich harmonies. The camera alternates between close-ups of the leads and wide shots of the ensemble, capturing their dynamic footwork and rhythmic clapping. Around the 0:35 mark, the Female Lead Performer takes over lead vocals, singing "And you got a little love in your life. They don't have to be running around and around and around" with a clear, melodic voice, while the Female Performer in Black Top and White Skirt becomes increasingly prominent, smiling and gesturing expressively near the front. The Male Lead Performer then returns to lead vocals, delivering expressive lines such as "I won't leave your love astray" with vocal runs and ad-libs, before the group collectively builds to a powerful, unified finale with sustained vocalizations and rhythmic "push, pull" sounds, maintaining high energy throughout the consistent studio backdrop with its arched doorways and large round clock.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a spacious rehearsal studio, featuring a high-energy a cappella performance by 'Pitch Slapped' from the '2014 International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella,' as indicated by on-screen text. The Group of Performers, dressed in coordinated outfits, executes synchronized dance moves with dynamic arm gestures, providing a driving, rhythmic vocal percussion and strong harmonies. The Male Lead Performer takes center stage, singing passionately with a clear, energetic voice, delivering lines like "fill me up until there's nothing left" and "let us take back tonight," while leading the choreography with sharp, wide arm extensions. The Female Lead Performer dances in sync with him, her black dress and statement necklace catching the light. As the camera pans across the tightly formed, energetic group, the Berklee College of Music Logo remains visible in the bottom right. Midway, the group transitions into a structured circle formation, with the Male Lead Performer at the center, where he delivers a powerful vocal line, "You gon' mess around and find out there ain't no one better," supported by the unified backing vocals. The Female Performer in Black Top and White Skirt becomes more prominent, smiling and gesturing toward the camera. The natural lighting highlights their movements. The energetic a cappella then shifts dramatically around 0:39, as the Male Lead Performer begins a melancholic, soulful solo, singing "I am feeling so small... I know nothing at all," accompanied by very sparse, soft, sustained vocal harmonies from the group, creating a stark emotional contrast.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with the Berklee College of Music Logo and on-screen text 'Pitch Slapped' and '2014 International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella' establishing the context. A close-up of the Female Lead Performer, in a black dress and statement necklace, captures her singing the emotional lead vocals "Stumbling, fall... I'm still learning to love" with expressive hand gestures. The camera then pans to the Male Lead Performer, dressed in a white shirt, black tie, and suspenders, who joins with his clear, melodic lead vocal "Just starting to crawl." They are positioned at the front of a tightly arranged Group of Performers in a spacious Studio Environment with wooden floors, white walls, arched doorways, and a large round clock. The entire ensemble provides rich, harmonized a cappella backing vocals, forming a full instrumental sound throughout the performance. The camera alternates between close-ups of the leads and wider shots of the group, highlighting their synchronized movements and dynamic facial expressions. The Female Lead Performer continues her powerful and emotional lead, delivering lines like "Say something, I'm giving up on you" and "Sorry that I couldn't get to you," while maintaining eye contact with the camera and interacting with the Male Lead, who gestures expressively. Midway, a Female Performer in a Black Top and White Skirt becomes a prominent focal point, smiling and gesturing enthusiastically as the group maintains high energy and choreographed vocal movements, supporting the Female Lead's continued powerful delivery of "Anywhere I would have followed you" and "Say something, I'm giving up on you," before the minute concludes with her singing "And I will..."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a bright, professional studio environment, a spacious rehearsal room with wooden floors, white walls, a large round clock above a fireplace, and arched doorways. The Berklee College of Music logo is consistently displayed in the bottom right corner. Initially, soft, sustained vocalizations create an atmospheric opening, accompanied by on-screen text identifying the group 'Pitch Slapped' and the '2014 International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella'. The Male Lead Performer and Female Lead Performer stand center stage, facing each other, as the Group of Performers forms a semi-circle behind them. A male voice then distinctly sings "Say something." The Male Lead Performer initiates choreography with expressive hand gestures and facial expressions, mirrored precisely by the Female Lead Performer. As upbeat, rhythmic a cappella music begins with strong vocal percussion and a bassline, a female voice takes the lead, singing catchy lines like "Come on over, come on over baby." The group transitions into dynamic, synchronized dance moves, with the Male Lead Performer stepping forward and the Female Lead Performer moving to his side, continuing her lead vocals with lyrics such as "Hey boy, don't you know I got something going on." The camera pans across the ensemble, highlighting their energy and coordination. The performers are dressed in coordinated outfits—men in white shirts, black ties, and suspenders; women in black tops or dresses with varied accessories. Midway through, the focus shifts to the Female Performer in Black Top and White Skirt, who becomes a prominent focal point, smiling and gesturing expressively as she moves to the front, contributing to the vibrant group performance. The choreography intensifies with sharp, rhythmic steps and arm movements, maintaining tight formation. The female lead vocals continue with lines like "I know you know, so baby don't pretend you want to keep me guessing if you..." and "Don't wanna play this game with you baby," supported by the group's harmonious backing vocals, vocal percussion, and bassline, conveying a strong sense of unity and performance excellence throughout the energetic atmosphere. The natural lighting remains consistent, enhancing the scene's clarity.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the On-Screen Text 'Pitch Slapped' and '2014 International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella' setting the stage for a dynamic a cappella performance by a group of young performers from Berklee College of Music, whose logo remains visible in the bottom right. In a spacious rehearsal studio with wooden floors and white walls, the Female Lead Performer, in a white top and black pants, takes center stage, singing the lead 'You text me, saying you want me for back, please' with expressive gestures and energetic movements. The Group of Performers, in coordinated outfits, provides intricate backing harmonies and rhythmic vocalizations, executing synchronized dance moves. As the Female Lead continues her powerful vocal delivery, 'But what you waiting for? It's open house, why don't you take a tour?', the camera captures various angles, highlighting their theatrical expressions. The Male Lead Performer, in a white shirt and suspenders, joins in, mirroring her movements and adding vocal intensity as the ensemble transitions into a powerful chorus, with voices blending on lines like 'I've been waiting for you' and 'Give it to you.' The Female Lead then returns to a melodic lead, 'But I won't have to wonder, baby,' before the Male Lead takes over with a distinct lead vocal, 'What could you drive me crazy? Take me for a ride.' The performance maintains high energy, with the Female Lead again singing, 'But I won't have to wonder, if you just come on over.' Finally, a Female Performer in a black top and white skirt, who has become increasingly prominent, delivers a clear, enthusiastic lead line, 'Don't you want to be the one tonight?', as the group concludes with a final rhythmic vocalization.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a spacious, well-lit rehearsal studio with wooden floors and white walls, featuring arched doorways and a large round clock, as a dynamic a cappella performance begins. The Female Lead Performer, in a white top and black pants with a statement necklace, takes center stage, leading the choreography with expressive hand gestures and vocal delivery, singing lines like "Don't you want to be just you and me?" and "We can do what comes naturally." The Male Lead Performer, in a white shirt, black tie, and suspenders, sings with animated facial expressions and gestures, often interacting with the Female Lead Performer, contributing to the energetic vocal arrangement. The Group of Performers, dressed in coordinated outfits—men in white shirts, black ties, and suspenders; women in black tops or patterned pants—execute synchronized dance moves with high energy, forming tight formations while providing powerful harmonies and vocal percussion, repeatedly chanting "What a girl likes, what a girl wants" and the central theme "All I want is you." The Berklee College of Music Logo remains consistently visible in the bottom right corner. As the performance progresses, the camera captures various angles, including close-ups of the Female Performer in Black Top and White Skirt, who becomes a prominent focal point with her smiling, expressive gestures, singing along to the building intensity of "All I want is you." The group transitions into a final pose, pointing directly at the camera, before the screen fades to black with on-screen text options: 'Credits', 'Subscribe', and 'Watch more from Berklee', as the a cappella performance concludes with a final flourish.

segments.integrated_caption: The video transitions from a live performance to a closing credits sequence, accompanied by a continuous, upbeat instrumental pop/rock music track. Initially, three small video thumbnails appear at the top of the screen, showcasing diverse musical performances: a woman singing into a microphone with a 'bim' logo, a man singing into a vintage-style microphone on stage, and hands playing a keyboard. Below these, white text boxes reading 'Credits', 'Subscribe', and 'Watch more from Berklee serve as a call-to-action, all underscored by the energetic music. The thumbnails then cycle through various performers, including a woman singing with a man in a hat, a man singing on stage under a spotlight, and a drummer playing in a dimly lit setting. The scene subsequently fades to black, and white text credits begin to roll, listing song titles such as 'Take Our Time', 'Radio Song', 'Ain't it Fun', and 'Take Back the Night', along with lead vocalists and arrangers. Additional credits follow, naming the members of 'Pitch Slapped' by vocal range, choreographers, recording engineers, videographer, and mastering studio. The Berklee College of Music logo is no longer visible as the environment shifts entirely to a black screen with the rolling text, marking the end of the performance and the beginning of the closing sequence, with the instrumental music providing a consistent, lively backdrop.
